payload.subject: B5 work order — bifactor model on v37-scored data (turn 30)
payload.summary: The bifactor precondition from turn 25 now holds: v37 provides a stable Sonnet-only foundation. This work order specifies the bifactor model fitting. Execute AFTER v37 deployment is confirmed and B3 steps 5-6 are complete.

work_order.sequencing: After v37 deployment confirmed AND B3 steps 5-6 complete. Bifactor should run on v37-scored (or v37-calibrated) data to ensure the factor structure reflects the production model.

specification.name: Prepare bifactor input data
specification.detail: Extract all 10 PSQ dimension scores for the Sonnet-scored corpus (n≈6,773 texts). Use LLM labels (Sonnet), not DistilBERT predictions. The bifactor model characterizes the construct structure of the rating system, not the student model's approximation of it.

specification.name: Fit bifactor model — 4-component specification
specification.detail: Fit a confirmatory bifactor model (Reise, 2012) with the following structure: (a) g-factor: all 10 dimensions load on g. (b) Bipolar specific factor: TE, HI, AD load positive (threat pole); RC, RB, TC, CC load negative (protection pole). (c) DA singleton specific factor: DA loads alone. (d) CO singleton specific factor: CO loads alone. (e) ED: allow cross-loading — fit two versions: ED on bipolar factor (negative loading hypothesized) and ED free to load on its own specific factor. Compare fit.
specification.software: Use lavaan (R) or semopy/factor_analyzer (Python). If neither available, compute omega coefficients directly from the correlation matrix using the Schmid-Leiman decomposition as an approximation.

specification.name: Report fit statistics
specification.detail: Report: CFI (≥0.95 good, ≥0.90 acceptable), RMSEA (≤0.06 good, ≤0.08 acceptable), SRMR (≤0.08 good). Compare against: (a) 1-factor model (g only), (b) the 4-component bifactor, (c) a 10-factor uncorrelated model (saturated baseline). Chi-square difference test between 1-factor and bifactor.

specification.name: Compute omega coefficients
specification.detail: omega_h = proportion of total reliable variance attributable to g. omega_s for each specific factor = proportion attributable to that factor. If omega_s > 0.10 for any specific factor, the dimension profile adds reliable information beyond g. This directly tests the criterion-validity finding: does the structural model confirm that profile shape carries independent signal?

specification.name: DA paradox test
specification.detail: Report DA's g-loading, specific-factor loading, and communality. If DA has low g-loading but high specific-factor loading, this explains the paradox from turn 25: DA predicts criteria through signal orthogonal to g. If DA has high g-loading and low specific loading, the paradox requires a different explanation.

work_order.deliverable: Report containing: bifactor model specification, fit statistics (CFI, RMSEA, SRMR), chi-square comparison vs 1-factor, omega_h, omega_s per specific factor, per-dimension loadings (g + specific), ED placement resolution, DA paradox test result.

action_gate.gate_condition: v37 deployment confirmed + B3 steps 5-6 complete
action_gate.gate_note: Do not begin until v37 is deployed and calibration re-fit is done. The bifactor analysis uses LLM labels (not DistilBERT predictions), but sequencing ensures all model artifacts are stable before adding new analytical work.

epistemic_flags: The 4-component bifactor specification derives from the B4 partial correlation analysis (inductive). The confirmatory bifactor model treats this as a hypothesis to test, not a confirmed structure. Poor fit (CFI < 0.90) would indicate the partial-correlation-derived structure does not hold under formal modeling.

Bifactor models with singleton factors (DA, CO) can be empirically underidentified. If the model fails to converge or produces Heywood cases (negative variance estimates), the singleton factors should be dropped and the model re-fit as g + bipolar only.

Using LLM labels rather than DistilBERT predictions means the bifactor structure characterizes Sonnet's rating behavior, not the text features that DistilBERT learns. These may differ — the student model compresses the rating space (B3 finding). A follow-up bifactor on DistilBERT predictions would test whether the compression preserves the factor structure.
